key_insight: Domain-specific biomedical transformer embeddings, especially BioBERT, improved detection of dosing-error risk in clinical trial protocols.
clinical_relevance: This approach could help flag potentially unsafe trial dosing earlier, supporting trial safety review and regulatory oversight.
method: Encoded trial text with ClinicalBERT, PubMedBERT, BioBERT, and MedCPT.
Combined embeddings with structured categorical metadata.
Tested classical ML and neural models, including logistic regression, SVC, gradient boosting, and residual neural networks.
evidence_signal: BioBERT achieved ROC-AUC 0.794 under logistic regression, and the strongest models reached ROC-AUC 0.821 to 0.853 for predicting dosage error.
watch_item: Performance was evaluated on trial text classification, so real-world deployment would still need external validation and careful integration into clinical workflows.
paper_title: CaresAI at CT-DEB26: Detecting Dosing Errors In Clinical Trials Using Domain-Specific Transformer Embeddings and Classification Models
